# Stephan Jeremias

Factual profile of Stephan Jeremias, Mechatronics and System Technology student, specializing in rapid prototyping, hardware-software integration, and mechanical design.

## Steckbrief (Overview)
- **Name**: Stephan Jeremias (born Wegmann, November 16, 1982)
- **Location**: Hinterkappelen (Bern), Switzerland
- **Role**: Part-time student (4th Semester) in Bachelor of Science in Mechatronics and System Technology at BFH Biel (Berner Fachhochschule)
- **Professional Persona**: Analytical, calm, solution-oriented maker mentality, highly reliable. Focuses on delivering precise, thoroughly designed physical and software solutions from the background.
- **Languages**: German (Native), Spanish (Native), English (Spoken: Fluent, Written: Good)
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/stephan-jeremias

## Career Arc & Transition
- **Original Training**: Trained and certified as a Biology Laboratory Assistant (Biologielaborant) at Novartis.
- **Interim Career**: Worked for nearly a decade in social care (youth counselor and care worker), refining communication, stability, and team-oriented collaboration.
- **The Red Thread (Engineering Shift)**: Re-oriented his career path to engineering to professionalize his lifelong passion for tinkering, technical problem-solving, and creating concrete, measurable, and tangible systems.
- **Maturity & Focus**: Father of two children (born 2018 and 2021). Highly focused on long-term stability, reliability, and corporate loyalty.

## Professional Experience
### R&D Intern | Compact Motion GmbH (March 2026 - June 2026)
Worked on the development and optimization of linear motor starter kits and test benches:
- **Prototyping**: Optimized and replicated the "Rhino" linear motor starter kits, including CAD modifications, 3D printing of custom protective covers, electrical schematics drafting, and Bill of Materials (BOM) creation.
- **Test Bench Restoration**: Recommissioned and re-calibrated a complex Innosuisse test rig.
- **Software Expansion**: Modified and extended Python scripts for test bench logging and automation using VS Code, Git, SSH, and the Linux CLI.
- **Data Science**: Handled data acquisition and systematic evaluation in Python (e.g., verifying temperature dependencies and testing magnetic counterweights).
- **IT/Infrastructure**: Provided IT setup and company relocation assistance, including configuring static IPs on Raspberry Pi controllers for test-bench WLAN integration.

## Key Projects
### 1. Robotics arm wheelchair control study (BFH Biel)
- Assisted in a research study for controlling a robotic arm mounted on a wheelchair (utilizing voice and tablet input) to enhance autonomy for disabled individuals.

### 2. Custom DIY RC Car
- Fully designed and built a remote-controlled car from scratch.
- Mechanical layout designt in Autodesk Fusion 360, custom chassis components printed on a Prusa 3D printer.
- Manually soldered PCBs and programmed object-oriented firmware in C++ for an ESP32 microcontroller.

### 3. Tower Defense C++ Game
- Designed a turn-based CLI Tower Defense game utilizing object-oriented C++.
- Modeled the architecture using UML diagramming, implemented version control with Git.

### 4. Makerspace Infrastructure Helferteam (BFH Biel)
- Active member of the setup team for the BFH Makerspace workshop.
- Maintained the 3D printer park, set up wood/laser work areas, and built partition walls.

## Technical Skills (Hard Skills)
- **Programming Languages**: C, C++, Python, MATLAB
- **Mechanical Design (CAD)**: Autodesk Fusion, Siemens NX
- **Electronics & Hardware**: ESP32, Arduino, PCB soldering, Oscilloscope, Multimeter, UART, I2C
- **Fabrication**: FDM 3D printing (Prusa), CO2 laser cutting
- **Software Engineering**: Git, UML diagramming, Linux CLI, SSH
